question_frequency.rationale: Three explicit OPTIONS forks (encode strategy, decode strategy, test suite), each at a meaningful decision point. The pair consistently engaged the user at decision moments rather than just writing code.

question_quality.rationale: The encode (manual vs groupby) and decode (regex vs slice) choices target real trade-offs: idiom/laziness and robustness/format-validation. The test-suite choice is somewhat lighter (more vs fewer cases) but still pedagogically useful.

scaffolding.rationale: Logical progression from encode → decode → tests, with follow-up explanations (groupby laziness, \d+ greediness) tied to user questions. Difficulty gradient is smooth; no spoilers given before user committed to choices.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Natural back-and-forth: user asks clarifying questions, tutor answers crisply and ties back to principles. Tone is warm without being saccharine, and each turn produces actionable progress.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code is correct, passes all five test cases including edge cases, and is equivalent to the baseline implementation. No notable advantage or disadvantage versus baseline.

friction.rationale: Took 4 turns vs baseline's 2, ~80s vs ~60s, but the extra length delivered three substantive choice points and good explanations of groupby laziness and regex greediness — proportional teaching payoff for modest extra length.
